content:
# Dorea v2 Architecture: Hybrid Rust + PyTorch Pipeline (2026-04-01)

## Decision
Rewrite dorea as a Rust binary with PyTorch inference subprocess. Not pure Rust — PyTorch is essential for video-aware models and future model flexibility.

## Why Keep PyTorch
- Video Depth Anything (CVPR 2025 Highlight) requires PyTorch — temporal state can't ONNX-export
- torch.compile gives fused kernel optimizations for repeated inference
- Batched inference, CUDA graphs, FP16 auto mixed precision
- Fine-tuning on footage, easy model swaps without re-export
- ONNX Runtime lags TensorRT and PyTorch on deeper networks

## Architecture

```
dorea (Rust binary)
├── CLI + orchestration (Rust, clap)
├── Video I/O (Rust + ffmpeg subprocess)
├── LUT building (Rust ndarray) — 50-100x faster than Python
├── GPU grading pipeline (Rust + wgpu compute shaders)
│   ├── Depth-stratified LUT apply (fused)
│   ├── Global HSL qualifier correction (fused)
│   └── Depth-aware ambiance grading (fused)
│
└── Model inference (Python subprocess, torch.compile optimized)
    ├── Video Depth Anything — temporal consistent depth, ~30fps
    ├── RAUNE-Net — color correction on keyframes only
    └── Communication: shared memory / named pipes for frames
```

## Pipeline Flow

### One-time setup (per scene)
1. Extract keyframes (Rust + ffmpeg)
2. RAUNE-Net on keyframes at proxy resolution (PyTorch) — color reference pairs
3. Build depth-stratified 33^3 LUTs (Rust) — σ=0, NN fill, gentle object weighting
4. Derive global HSL qualifier corrections (Rust) — 6-vector DaVinci-style

### Per-frame
5. Video Depth Anything at proxy resolution (PyTorch) — temporal consistent depth
6. Upscale depth map to 4K (bilinear, fast)
7. Apply depth-stratified LUT + HSL + depth grading (Rust+wgpu fused compute shader)
8. Encode output (Rust + ffmpeg NVENC)

## Proxy Resolution Strategy
VDA and RAUNE-Net can run at smaller resolution (512-1024px) when processing 4K video.
- Depth maps: compute at proxy, bilinear upscale to 4K — depth is low-frequency, upscale is fine
- RAUNE-Net: runs on keyframes only at proxy — LUT captures the color mapping, applied at 4K
- LUT apply + HSL + grading: runs at native 4K on GPU — these are per-pixel ops, cheap
- **Benchmark needed**: quantify quality degradation at 512px vs 1024px proxy for both models

## 4K Performance Estimate (5-min clip, 30fps = 9000 frames)

| Step | Engine | Per-frame | Notes |
|------|--------|-----------|-------|
| Video decode | Rust+ffmpeg | ~3ms | NVDEC hardware |
| Video Depth Anything | PyTorch (proxy) | ~15-33ms | 512-1024px, temporal |
| RAUNE-Net | PyTorch (proxy) | ~50ms | Keyframes only (~75 total) |
| LUT+HSL+grade (fused) | Rust+wgpu | ~3ms | Native 4K, GPU compute |
| Video encode | Rust+ffmpeg | ~10ms | NVENC hardware |
| **Per-frame total** | | **~31-49ms** | **20-32 FPS at 4K** |

## What's Dropped from Original Pipeline
- Phase 0 (old LUT gen) — replaced by RAUNE-Net + depth-stratified LUT
- Phase 2 (Claude scene analysis) — was for SAM2 initialization, no longer needed
- Phase 3 (SAM2 tracking) — depth maps provide sufficient object separation
- Phase 5 (DaVinci Resolve) — replaced by code-based GPU grading

## What's Kept
- Phase 1 (frame extraction) — ported to Rust+ffmpeg
- Phase 4 (depth estimation) — upgraded to Video Depth Anything
- pipeline_utils.py D-Log M functions — ported to Rust

## Cargo Workspace
```
dorea/
  crates/
    dorea-cli/          # Binary: CLI + pipeline orchestration
    dorea-core/         # LUT build, HSL derivation, color math
    dorea-gpu/          # wgpu compute shaders: fused grading pipeline
    dorea-video/        # ffmpeg subprocess: decode/encode
  python/
    dorea_inference/    # PyTorch inference server
      vda.py            # Video Depth Anything
      raune.py          # RAUNE-Net
      server.py         # Frame I/O via shared memory
  models/               # Model weights (gitignored)
```

## Key Risks
1. wgpu compute shader for Gaussian blur (clarity) — needs separable 2-pass with shared memory
2. Python subprocess latency — mitigated by shared memory, async frame pipeline
3. Video Depth Anything on RTX 3060 6GB — may need smallest model variant
4. Proxy resolution degradation — needs benchmarking at 512/768/1024px
